mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate triggers a decrease in labor demand, leading to lower supply and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in post-recovery periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the housing market triggers a decrease in demand for goods and services, leading to lower supply and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 units in 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in housing-market-down periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production rate signals an expansion of supply, leading to higher demand and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the consumer price index signals a decrease in inflation expectations, leading to lower interest rates and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the payroll employment rate signals an increase in labor demand, leading to higher supply and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises above 160000 in 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate triggers a decrease in labor demand, leading to lower supply and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in post-recovery periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the retail sales rate signals an increase in demand, leading to higher supply and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 3.5% in 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
